description: Read top 30 stories from Hacker News front page. Extracts title, URL, domain, author, score, and comment count. No login required.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: news.ycombinator.com renders stories as pure HTML table rows — no React, no SPA. Each story consists of two <tr> rows: one with the titleline (.titleline > a for title+URL, .sitebit .sitestr for domain) and one with the subtext (.score for points, .hnuser for author, .age a for time, a:contains('comments') for comment count). Story rank is .rank. This is the simplest and most stable structure in SolaceBrowser.
reasoning.selector_strategy: Use .athing for the title row container. Then .subtext (next sibling) for metadata. .titleline > a gives href (URL) and text (title). .sitestr gives domain. .score span has points. .hnuser has username. Comments link has text pattern 'N comments'. HN ID: .athing[id] attribute.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: HN has NO bot detection for reads — explicitly crawler-friendly. No login needed. No rate limiting for normal page loads. 34KB page loads in <1s. Do not scrape more than 60 pages/minute as courtesy.
reasoning.performance: Navigation 2s + wait 1s + extraction 1s = ~4s total. Extremely fast.

page.description: Page number (each page has 30 stories). Default 1.

steps.description: Navigate to HN front page (no login required)
Return extracted stories as structured data

title.example: Show HN: I built a new programming language

error_handling.session_expired: HN does not require login for reads — this error should not occur
error_handling.empty_page: Return empty stories array — page number may be out of range
error_handling.selector_timeout: Retry once with 15s timeout, then abort
